Verify Custom Domain Configuration (if necessary): Ensure that your custom domain is correctly configured in Magento’s system settings under `System -> Domains` and also inside the database (`admin_url` field within core_config_data table). 5. Check Admin URL Syntax: Make sure you are using a valid syntax for admin access, e.g., http://yourcustomdomain.com/admin/ or https://yourcustomdomain.com/index.php/admin/ 6. Clear Cookies and Session Data (Sometimes): If the issue persists after clearing caches: - Clear all cookies from your browser. 7. Check if Admin Login is Enabled: Ensure that admin login functionality has not been disabled in `app/etc/config.xml` file. 8. Browser Incognito Mode (Sometimes): Try accessing the Magento admin panel using an incognito window to rule out any JavaScript-based errors or conflicts with existing browser extensions. If after following these steps you are still unable to login, it may be beneficial for further troubleshooting: - Check server logs and configuration files. - Verify that all necessary database fields have been updated correctly (including `admin_url` in core_config_data). By carefully going through each step above should help resolve the issue. texts.full_text: is largely a consequence of the symmetrical design of most mice, accompanied by the lack of either sculpting or sizing. Some mice such as Orthopedic Mice are not only asymmetric but also anatomically derived and support the bonny structures of the hand, reducing the grip effort. This is not the case with most mice. For example, the standard Microsoft IntelliMouse is not only symmetrical, but also has a relatively flat top surface, and is not anatomically derived. (The IntelliMouse Optical mouse has a slight concavity in the top surface, which is a step in the right direction, but the mouse is still symmetrical and not anatomically derived, and the concavity is not large enough to provide a firm support to the hand.) Consequently, the hand tends to slide off the IntelliMouse, which in turn requires the user to maintain a static grip to keep the hand in place. This grip is not a short-term grip. It is a grip that is maintained, with few if any short-term breaks, throughout the entire mousing session, which is a source of static grip fatigue. Mice that are not only asymmetrical, but also anatomically derived, such as the Orthopedic Mouse, are not only ergonomically shaped, but also do not require a static grip, and are therefore not a source of static grip fatigue. texts.full_text: Exorcizing the Ghost of Mental Imagery. Commentary on: JI Glasgow: "The Imagery Debate Revisited." Computational Intelligence (in press) EXORCIZING THE GHOST OF MENTAL IMAGERY Stevan Harnad Laboratoire Cognition et Mouvement Universite d'Aix Marseille II 13388 Marseille cedex 13, France harnad@cogsci.soton.ac.uk Janice Glasgow has presented some very interesting ideas and findings concerning array representations and the processing of data referring to visible objects, but the connection with imagery seems to be largely interpretive rather than substantive. The problem seems apparent even in Glasgow's term ``depict'', which is used by way of contrast with ``describe''. Now ``describe'' refers relatively unproblematically to strings of symbols, such as those in this written sentence, that are systematically interpretable as propositions describing objects, events, or states of affairs. But what does ``depict'' mean? Ican easily imagine a photograph depicting the Queen's head (say), but it is not clear at all how an array could depict anything other than itself: It would just be another string-like sequence that had to somehow end up being systematically interpretable as describing objects, events or states of affairs in order for any cognitive operations on them even make sense. texts.full_text: 01 Poydras Street in the Central Business District of New Orleans, Louisiana, is a 28-story, -tall high-rise building. Designed by Skidmore, Owings & Merrill, it was built in 1980 as the headquarters for the Pan-American Life Insurance Co. In December 2006, Pan American sold the building to Equastone, a California real estate company. The Hotel Interconteral opened there on September 1st of that year and began operating under its new name - "Pan American Center". It was operated by Marriott International until it closed in December 2007 due to low occupancy. In May 2013, Pan American Life Building became the headquarters for a private equity firm called HCI Equity Partners. The building is known as one of New Orleans' most iconic high-rises

texts.full_text: Q: Analytic Function vs Exponential Order function We say that a function $f$ is of exponential order $\alpha$ if there exist constants: $M$, $\alpha$, $T$ such that for $x>T$ $$f(x)<M\cdot e^{\alpha x}The concept you're pointing to relates the growth rate comparison between functions, specifically using an analytic function or any given function \( f \) with polynomial order. A function is said to be of "exponential order" if its long-term behavior does not evolve more than a certain multiple of some polynomial function. To formalize this: A real-valued constant (or measurable on the negative reals, for example in Laplace transform theory contexts), \( r(z) \), defined over an interval starting from 0 or any other point up to infinity is said to be of order at most α if there exist parameters M > 0 and T ≥ 0 such that: \[ |f(t)| < Me^{\alpha t} \quad\text{for all }t>T.\] Here, \(M\) operates as a scaling factor while \(\alpha>0\) controls the growth rate. This definition defines an upper bound on how fast function parameters can evolve relative to some exponential curve. Analytic functions are those that have derivatives of every order in their domain and so they admit Taylor series expansions around any point within this region which converge back to them (in a neighborhood). They also exhibit smoothness properties but the rate at which these functions rise or decline can not be compared against exponentials using above criteria. For instance, integers grow less than linear function as \(x \to +\infty\) and hence are of order 0 in this context. The difference between an analytic property (such as having a Taylor series representation) versus being bounded by some polynomial expansion is necessary: not all functions that do or don't have these properties will not be classified

texts.full_text: substrate, followed by patterning of the nano-sized feature structures through exposure to ultraviolet light. 3 Method for Manufacturing Fine Patterns Embodiments described herein present an alternative method that combines advances in photolithography with innovative materials and processes. The novel manufacturing process is designed specifically targeting small-scale components where precise control over dimensions becomes critical due either from the physical limitations of current technology or strategic design considerations within device architectures. The core technique involves depositing a thin film layer onto substrates which can support nanometer-sized structures, using techniques like atomic-layer deposition (ALD) to ensure molecular-level precision in material composition and thickness. Following this initial step is an exposure process under controlled conditions where the surface of that deposited films undergoes selective changes due UV radiation. A photomask with desired pattern details would then be placed over or directly onto these altered areas during light illumination, enabling precise replication down to nanoscale levels using deep ultraviolet (DUV) lasers. Post-exposure development processes involve the use of chemical solvents which etch away unwanted regions from beneath and above where no UV-induced changes have occurred. Subsequently a metal deposition step is carried out wherein targeted areas with patterns are further processed to ensure robustness, conductivity or other specific material properties required by design parameters. Finally an additional process like wet-chemical treatments might be performed for improving adhesion between the substrate materials and those deposited metals structures in addition enhancing pattern sharp edges. Through this approach not only is a high degree of control achieved over feature sizes within micrometer to nanometer scales, but also efficiency improvements are anticipated owing better useable surface areas per total deposition. texts.full_text: l Master, afterOlaf Barda, in 1961. He won four Norwegian chess championships, in 1959, 1962, 1970 and 1973. According to ChessBase, Johannessen had a wideopening repertoire. He played most of the regular opening moves with White with some frequency, 1.d4, 1.e4, 1.c4 and 1.Nf3. With Black against 1.e4 he frequently entered the "open" games with2... f6 or 2... d5, whileagainst1.d4 he often chosethe Queen's Gambit (1... d5) and the Scandinavian Defense (1... d3). This flexibility allowed him to surprise opponents with less common variations and kepthis gamesdynamic and complex. Johannessen was known for his strategic thinking, strong endgame skills, and ability to play well under pressure. His contributions to Norwegian chess were not only through his personal achievements but also as a coach and mentor to many aspiring players. He worked tirelessly to promote the game in Norway, contributing significantly to its growth in popularity during hislifetime. Beyond his chess career, Svein Johannessen was a respected teacher and author. He wrote several books

texts.full_text: non-fancy counterparts. They prefer horizontal space rather than vertical depth in a tank or aquarium, which is why they tend to swim near the surface. Their preference for swimming at top portions of tanks can sometimes indicate that there's not enough room horizontally. In smaller tanks, these fish may congregate on one side as it provides them with more mobility and territory compared to other areas. However, this behavior could also be a sign of stress or discomfort due to overcrowding or inadequate water conditions. If your friend notices her goldfishes mostly swimming at the top left portion consistently, she should consider checking tank size, water quality parameters such as temperature, pH levels, ammonia and nitrate concentrations. It's important for their well-being that they have enough space to swim freely without stress from overcrowding or poor living conditions. The recommendation of keeping these types in at least 70-gallon aquariums is based on providing them with a suitable environment conducive to their swimming habits, growth needs and overall health. Can someone please describe the usual upgrade process for PHP when compiling manually? Is there anything that has to be removed or changed from the previous version installed? I'm clueless. Please help! Edit: I think the general consensus is back-up, try it on a test server, and document everything. Thanks for all your feedback! A: is it just as easy as downloading the newest source, uncompressing it, compiling it using the same (or comparable) options that were used on the last version? That may Upgrading PHP from 5.3.3 to 5.3.4 by compiling manually is generally straightforward, but requires careful attention to detail. Here's a step-by-step guide to help you through the process: --- ### 1. Backup Everything - Back up your current PHP installation: Save your existing `php.ini`, configuration files, and any custom scripts. Example: ```bash cp /usr/local/lib/php.ini /path/to/backup/ ``` - Back up your web server configuration: Ensure you can restore Apache/Nginx settings if needed. - Back up your website data: Protect your applications in case of unexpected issues. --- ### 2. texts.full_text: We live in a time where people are truly perplexed over what has gone wrong with our world—a world that seems increasingly divided and morally ambiguous. This confusion is often exacerbated by contrasting views on morality, especially when considering the perspectives of atheists versus those who believe in higher powers or divine guidance. The question "Morality and Atheism: Who Makes The Rules?" delves into this complex issue to explore how different worldviews shape our understanding—and practice—of what is right from wrong. As society becomes increasingly secular, with many turning away from traditional religious beliefs that have historically guided moral decisions for centuries or even millennia. In a purely atheistic framework where no divine entity presides over human affairs and morality isn't dictated by any sacred texts of faiths such as Christianity, Islam etc., the question arises: who then sets these standards? Is it humanity itself through collective consensus on what constitutes right behavior? For many secular individuals or societies influenced heavily in their moral reasoning from a scientific perspective (often referred to as humanism), morality is grounded within principles that promote happiness and well-being of all. This can be seen reflected across various philosophical traditions such like utilitarian ethics, which prioritize actions resulting greatest good for the largest number. However this approach may not satisfy those who hold strong religious convictions where they believe moral laws are divinely ordained by God or gods as per their sacred texts—such beliefs have historically been a cornerstone of many societies worldwide and

texts.full_text: can be explained by the withdrawal of progesterone. This steroid imposes a profound inhibition of LH pulse frequency in the luteal phase. Experimental ewes were ovariectomized in the late luteal phase of the estrous cycle and divided into three groups: 1) no estradiol provided; 2...and 3) with exogenous administration of estradiol, at a dose that mimicked natural levels. The ewes were then allowed to progress through their follicular phases as normally. Throughout the experiment, blood samples were collected regularly from each group to measure luteinizing hormone (LH), progesterone, and estradiol concentrations in order to monitor changes in LH pulse frequency, serum steroid profiles, and reproductive status. The results showed that ewes receiving exogenous estradiol exhibited a significant increase in the frequency of LH pulses during their follicular phases compared with those not treated with estradioL or ovariectomized without any hormone replacement. One of the patients Heparin-induced Thrombocytopenia in Pediatric Intensive Care Unit Population Introduction: The incidence and severity of HIT are not well documented among pediatric patients, especially those within a critical care setting such as an intensive care unit (ICU). This article aims to highlight the occurrence of heparin induced thombocytopenia(HTI) in two paediatric ICU cases at our institution. It also discusses its pathophysiology and outlines management approaches for suspected HTI within this vulnerable population. Case Descriptions: The pediatric intensive care unit (ICU), located in a tertiary-care center, observed HIT among 2 patients - one aged about two months receiving heparin as the flush solution through their central line catheter due to prolonged hypercoagulable state and another child around six month old who had been administered full-dose of intravenous unfractionated heparin (IVUH) during a cardio-pulmonary bypass surgery. Both children previously received Heparin injections as neonates for suspected sepsis, but thrombocytopenia was not considered to be due to HTI. The diagnosis required specific tests like the Hepler-induced platelet aggregation study which confirmed both cases of heparin induced thombocytopenium. Management: The treatment approach included cessation or discontinuation of Heparin administration, with careful observation and supportive care for any concurrent complications such as bleeding due to low levels of thrombocytes. texts.full_text: the federal government offers financial incentives for adopting better flood mitigation practices, while also potentially reducing disaster assistance funding if these standards are not met. In August 2018, President Trump signed into law the Disaster Recovery Reform Act (DRRA) as part of a larger FAA reauthorization bill. This act made significant changes to how FEMA operates and responds to disasters: - It allows for pre-disaster hazard mitigation grants which can be used before an event occurs. - the DRRA also increases federal assistance available through Public Assistance, making it easier for communities to recover from natural disasters by covering a higher percentage of costs associated with debris removal and emergency protective measures. These legislative actions indicate that there was indeed movement towards prioritizing flood risk management at the national level. However, whether these changes will be sufficient or effectively implemented remains an open question as we move forward into future years where climate change is expecteD to exacerbate flooding risks across many regions

texts.full_text: hile the rest of the content is gated behind a one-time, $10 purchase. Worlds 1-1 through 1-4 are playable at no cost, as are trials of the World Tour, Toad Rally and Kingdom Builder modes. Aside from the $10 (and unlike in Nintendo’s recent Fire Emblem: Heroes), the game contains no microtransactions. The iPhone giant finally brings its iconic mascot to Android devices, continuing Mario's journey beyond consoles and into the gaming space. Critics have noted that this move signifies Nintendo's commitment to expanding its reach in the rapidly growing mobile market, aiming to capture a larger share of smartphone users who enjoy casual gaming on their devices. While some fans may lament the shift towards paid content within mobile games, Super Mario Run maintains a clean record compared to other titles that have been criticized for aggressive monetization tactics. By offering a significant portion of the game for free and allowing players to unlock all levels through a single purchase, Nintendo demonstrates its dedication to preserving the integrity of both its brand and beloved character franchises like Mario. texts.full_text: y. That is why companies need employees that have a wide scope and an interdisciplinary approach to business. The Master’s programme in Imagineering at NHTV Breda University of Applied Sciences aims to fill the shortage of these interdisciplinary specialists. Students learn to design new products, services, and processes within companies and society. They learn to approach traditional values with innovative ideas while also creating new values. This programme is taught in English, lasts 1 year, and requires a Bachelor’s degree in Business Administration or a related field to apply. The programme’s graduates receive a professional Master of Business Administration diploma in Imagineering. Examples of potential jobs after graduation include Policy & Innovation Specialist, Strategic Brand Innovations Manager, co-creation consultant, and project manager. In the Keuzegids Masters 2016 ranking, the Master’s programme in Imagineering scored above average in its contents, communication, lecturers, scientific education, and facilities. Student feedback Here is how students of the Master’s programme in Imagineering describe their experience at NHTV Breda University. Ricky van Rensen, the Netherlands: "I was searching for those different perspectives, tools and ideas for situations in society and businesses where the old thinking did not proved to be suffice anymore. Imagineering turned out to provide me with all and much more of that. Not to mention the great classmates, lecturers and very much interesting topics we are having. This is a fun, useful and very inspiring master’s programme that helps people cope with complex issues in businesses and society. A programme where you discover possibilities, ways of thinking and a world out there of which you could have never imagined to have existed. A journey where you definitely are going to come out differently as you went in." Care Prarakkamo, Thailand: "NHTV has always been my first choice since I started to look for a master’s programme in the Netherlands. texts.full_text: than AIDS, malaria and measles combined ?Almost one-tenth of the disease burden could be prevented by improving water supply, sanitation, hygiene and management of water resources. Such improvements reduce child mortality and improve health and nutritional status in a sustainable way. LIFE IMPACT Poor access to safe water doesn’t just impact health, it also impacts how the poor must spend their time and the quality of their lives: Millions of women and children spend hours and can walk several miles a day collecting water from distant, often polluted sources. In just one day, more than 200 million hours of women’s time is consumed for the most basic of human needs — collecting water for domestic use. A 5-gallon bucket is a common item millions of people in the developing world use to collect, transport, and store their water. A gallon bucket of water weighs 42 pounds. A twelve-year-old African girl typically weighs 80 pounds. WATER USAGE AND ACCESS According to the World Health Organization, the requirement per person per day for adequate drinking and personal hygiene is 20 liters (5 gallons). Below this level people are constrained in their ability to maintain their physical well being. The average North American uses more than 100 gallons of water each day, but the average person in the developing world uses than three. texts.full_text: hronic low back pain were explored in the cross-sectional HUNT 2survey of a Norwegian county in 1995-1997, including 39,872 individuals who never used antihypertensive medication. A prospective study, comprising two decades of follow-up from this cohort, wasconducted to investigate whether high blood pressure is associated with reduced risk of developing chronic low back pain among those without previous history of the condition at baseline. It's also responsible our arousal state or consciousness level - : Located near midbrain, pons help relay messages from cerebellum to rest of the brain involving movements & posture while coordinating activities like chewing etc.. PONS is involved with REM sleep phase too!. texts.full_text: The telemetry and command antenna Employed on the Satellite Business Systems (SBS) satellite is also a bicone antenna but it operates only in the linearly polarized mode, and does not operate in the circularly polarized mode. Furthermore, the frequency bandwidth of conventional antennas is only about 2% of the center frequency. typically, the telemetry and command antennas are not used both for transmitting and receiving. Instead, separate transmit and receive antennas are used. Accordingly, it is an objective of the present invention to provide a circularlypolarized Ku-band telemetry and command bicone antenna that operates at three frequency channels. texts.full_text: as 'approximate sparsity'—essentially meaning the output distribution from these quantum circuits is heavily concentrated on only few possible states. This characteristic allows for an efficient classical simulation even though without this promise, simulating such circuit outputs would typically require resources that scale exponentially with system size. The significance of our findings extends beyond just theoretical computer science; it also has practical implications in the field where complex multi-qubit systems are becoming increasingly relevant—such as quantum computing and cryptography. In these fields specifically, proving or disproving certain conjectures about how difficult specific problems might be to solve classically can have profound impacts on both theory development but more importantly for applications. Moreover by identifying when a given circuit is likely sparse we could potentially enable the design of new algorithms that exploit this property and hence run much faster than traditional quantum simulations. This would represent an important step towards practical, large-scale simulation capabilities which are necessary to harness fully as classical computers evolve alongside their emerging Quantum counterparts.
